title: x-entity types
description: Registry of valid `x-entity` annotation values. Each value tags a schema field whose contents carry identity of the named entity type. The context-entity lint reads this registry to reject unknown `x-entity` values and to catch mismatches between storyboard capture and consume sites. See docs/contributing/x-entity-annotation.md for authoring guidance. To add a value, PR this file and extend the authoring doc.

x-entity-definitions.advertiser_brand: A brand in an advertiser's house portfolio. Identified by the advertiser — e.g., the `brand_id` on get_brand_identity, the `brand_id` inside core/brand-ref.json (buyer_brand, buyer), and core/account.json `brand`. If you `$ref` core/brand-id.json you are asserting advertiser scope; talent-roster ids must use a separate type even if the string shape is identical.
x-entity-definitions.rights_holder_brand: A brand in a rights agent's roster (talent, music artist, stock-media owner). Identified by the rights agent — e.g., `brand_id` on get_rights-response items, acquire_rights-response, and the rights-filter `brand_id` on get_rights-request.
x-entity-definitions.rights_grant: A rights grant identifier covering any lifecycle state (acquired, pending_approval, rejected). `rights_id` across brand/* schemas. Named `grant` rather than `contract` because the identifier is issued at request time — a bilateral contract may or may not exist yet.
x-entity-definitions.account: Billing/scope account in the seller's namespace. `account_id` or the natural-key `{brand, operator}` form; both resolve to the same entity.
x-entity-definitions.operator: An operator (seller) identity, typically by domain. Distinct from `account` — one operator issues many accounts.
x-entity-definitions.media_buy: A media buy / campaign. `media_buy_id` across media-buy/* schemas.
x-entity-definitions.package: A line item within a media buy. `package_id` across media-buy/* schemas.
x-entity-definitions.product: A seller inventory product. `product_id` across media-buy/* and core/product.json.
x-entity-definitions.pricing_option: A pricing tier selected from a product or rights offering. `pricing_option_id`.
x-entity-definitions.creative: A creative asset (library entry, buyer-assigned). `creative_id` across creative/*, brand/creative-approval-*, and media-buy/package-request.
x-entity-definitions.creative_format: A format spec identified by the composite of `agent_url` + `id` (see core/format-id.json).
x-entity-definitions.audience: A buyer-managed audience (CRM, lookalike seed, suppression). `audience_id` in media-buy/sync-audiences-request.
x-entity-definitions.signal: A data signal (catalog or agent-native). `signal_id` in signals/* schemas. Opaque activation ids use `signal_activation_id` instead.
x-entity-definitions.signal_activation_id: Opaque identifier used to activate a signal on a signals agent. `signal_agent_segment_id` in signals/* schemas. Scoped to the signals agent that issued it; not interchangeable with `signal`.
x-entity-definitions.event_source: A conversion pixel or event feed. `event_source_id` in media-buy/sync-event-sources-request, media-buy/log-event-request, and core/event.json.
x-entity-definitions.collection_list: A buyer-managed collection list. `list_id` on collection/* schemas.
x-entity-definitions.property_list: A buyer-managed property list. `list_id` on property/* schemas. Same field name as collection_list — annotation distinguishes them.
x-entity-definitions.catalog: A buyer catalog feed (product, inventory, promotion, offering). `catalog_id` in media-buy/sync-catalogs-request and core/catalog.json.
x-entity-definitions.property: A publisher property in the seller's view. Appears in property/* and referenced by core/property.json.
x-entity-definitions.media_plan: A media plan (distinct from media_buy — a plan can have multiple buys or precede a buy). `plan_id` in governance/sync-plans-request and related schemas when the scope is media planning.
x-entity-definitions.governance_plan: A governance plan (AI Act, consent, suitability). `plan_id` in governance/* schemas when the scope is governance, not media planning.
x-entity-definitions.content_standards: A content-standards document. `standards_id` in content-standards/* schemas.
x-entity-definitions.task: An async task handle (governance check, validation result). `task_id` across core/tasks-* schemas.
x-entity-definitions.si_session: A sponsored-intelligence conversation session. `session_id` in sponsored-intelligence/* schemas.
